top_risks: Job orchestration on Azure Storage Queue is a lost-job/duplicate-job dichotomy for 4-hour work: delete-on-receive means a crashed worker loses the message and the ACA retry restarts a container that polls an empty queue (job stuck 'running' until stale-fail, no real retry); keep-until-done means the default visibility timeout expires mid-job and KEDA spawns a duplicate GPU run (double COGS). No poison-message handling — a corrupt video burns 2 full GPU attempts (~$6-8) per failure. And 'atomically debits a credit, creates the job row, and drops a message' is not atomic: Postgres commit + queue send are two systems with no outbox.

Concrete timeout bug: ACA job timeout 5h vs the existing 4h stale-job auto-fail — a legitimate 4.5h job gets auto-failed and credit-refunded, then completes and writes results: user gets a free report plus a refund.

Throughput budget is unproven and likely calibrated at the top of their own range: $3.10 at ~$0.84/hr ACA T4 implies ~3.7h/job; InferenceSlicer multiplies detection 4-6x (~2h alone), BoT-SORT camera-motion compensation is CPU-bound on 4 vCPU, the full-match annotated 720p render pass is unbudgeted, and scale-from-zero adds image-pull + TensorRT engine-build minutes per job. Jobs plausibly run 4-5h, brushing the 5h kill and busting COGS.
Floor-price arithmetic error: (8 - 6.45)/8 = 19.4% margin, not '>50%' as claimed. The 50%-margin floor at worst-case COGS is $12.90.

Results-storage claim off ~50x: a full-match 720p H.264 preview is ~1-1.7GB, not 'tens of MB', retained Hot for the life of the account — unbounded storage growth, an egress line that covers about one viewing, and it quietly defeats the 90-day raw-deletion story because the preview IS recognizable footage of minors kept forever.

Minors'-data exposure: GET /players/<id>/video-reports is listed as public, surfacing video-derived performance data, heatmaps and (via the tab) imagery of academy children on a public page; no DPA/erasure/consent framework for children's footage or for opposition players processed by name without their club's knowledge.

Training-data provenance: a CC BY 4.0 tag on Roboflow Universe datasets does not clear the broadcast copyright in the underlying frames, and the UK TDM exception (CDPA s29A) is non-commercial only — directly relevant given the UK (Forest) market. Separately, the tracklet-merge 'appearance embedding' has no specified source; virtually all off-the-shelf person-ReID weights are trained on research-only or retracted datasets (Market-1501, DukeMTMC) — a licensing landmine the AGPL analysis missed.

Upload path holes: a 60-min SAS dies mid-upload for 6GB on typical club uplinks (~10-20 Mbps = 40-80+ min) with no re-mint endpoint; no size/duration cap before debit+enqueue (a 200GB blob or 8-hour file burns GPU); the write SAS remains valid after processing starts (content-swap TOCTOU, no ETag pinning); and ffmpeg decodes attacker-supplied bytes inside a worker holding Postgres credentials and a storage identity.

Tag-review labor is the unpriced product risk: with ID switches 'every few minutes', 22 players yield hundreds of merged tracklets; if review takes 60-90 minutes of coach time per $25 match the product fails regardless of CV quality — and Phase 0 measures CV accuracy but not tagging time.

Accuracy presentation contradicts the panning-camera physics: '8.2-9.0 km' implies full-match distance, but a panning single camera cannot observe players who are off-frame for long stretches — it is either on-camera-only distance mislabeled, or extrapolation (violating the project's own no-fabrication principle). 2-5 Hz keypoint homography interpolated across fast pans corrupts positions exactly when sprints happen.
Spot-path COGS ($0.69 ≈ 2.2h at ~$0.32/hr) assumes zero evictions; without stage checkpointing an eviction at hour 2.5 of 3 restarts from zero, inflating both cost and turnaround.
required_changes: Fix orchestration: either move to Azure Service Bus (peek-lock with lock renewal, dead-letter queue, max-delivery count) or keep Storage Queue but add: long visibility timeout with a renewal heartbeat, worker claims the job via conditional UPDATE on video_analysis_jobs (queued→running) so duplicates no-op, poison handling after N dequeues (fail, refund, alert), and an outbox/reconciler that re-enqueues 'queued' rows with no corresponding message instead of pretending debit+enqueue is atomic.

Align the timeouts: stale-job auto-fail must exceed the ACA job timeout (e.g., reconcile at 6h for a 5h job) and must check ACA job-execution state before refunding, so a still-running job is never refunded.
Fix the pricing table: raise the floor to ≥$13/match (or get worst-case COGS under ~$4) and include Stripe fees (2.9% + $0.30 ≈ $1.03 on a $25 single = ~4 margin points) in the margin math.

Make Phase 0 produce numbers, not vibes: a measured per-stage time budget on an actual T4 for a real 90-min 1080p match including InferenceSlicer and the full annotated render pass, plus a measured median tag-review duration with a non-engineer tagging. Set explicit go/no-go thresholds (e.g., job ≤3.5h, tagging ≤20 min).

Lock down minors' data: make /players/<id>/video-reports authenticated and team-scoped; apply the 90-day lifecycle (or Cool-then-delete) to preview_720p.mp4 and tracklet thumbnails, retaining only derived numbers/heatmaps long-term; define DPA terms with clubs as controllers, an erasure path, and a documented basis (or exclusion) for opposition players.

Harden the upload path: SAS re-mint endpoint for resumable uploads; server-side blob-size cap and ffprobe duration/codec validation BEFORE credit debit and enqueue; record blob ETag at upload-complete and verify at job start; run ffmpeg decode as an unprivileged subprocess isolated from the process holding DB credentials.

Close provenance gaps: specify the tracklet-merge embedder as license-clean (e.g., reuse the SigLIP crop embeddings already in the pipeline; explicitly ban research-only ReID weights); keep a dataset-provenance manifest; get counsel on training on broadcast-derived Universe frames given the UK TDM exception is non-commercial; shift fine-tunes toward consented customer footage fast; pin an LGPL-only ffmpeg build (NVENC, no libx264) in vision-image CI.

Fix the metrics honesty gap: report 'on-camera distance' explicitly paired with minutes-visible (never an implied full-match figure), and propagate homography between 2-5 Hz keypoint samples using the tracker's camera-motion-compensation affine rather than pure temporal smoothing.

Redo the storage line: preview_720p.mp4 is ~1-1.7GB, not 'tens of MB' — either lifecycle it with the raw video or render only a highlight strip plus per-tracklet clips; re-state per-match storage and egress with corrected sizes.
Gate the Spot migration on stage-level checkpointing (resume from persisted detections/positions parquet) so evictions don't restart 3h jobs from zero — otherwise $0.69 is fiction.

Kill cold-start COGS: bake prebuilt TensorRT engines into the image (or blob-cache keyed by GPU/driver) and enable artifact streaming — otherwise every scale-from-zero job pays 10-20 billed minutes of pull+build that the $0.05 'ops' line does not cover.

Support grassroots reality: multi-file uploads (two halves recorded separately is the norm) and pre/post-match + halftime trimming or play-segment detection — otherwise warmup footage silently poisons distance and minutes-visible.
cost_check: Compute rate (verified externally): ACA serverless T4 bills ~$0.00009/GPU-s (~$0.32/hr) + ~$0.35/hr for 4 vCPU + ~$0.17/hr for 16GiB ≈ $0.84/hr all-in. The claimed $3.10/match therefore implies ~3.7h/job — the very top of their own 1-4h range, with zero headroom for the InferenceSlicer multiplier (67,500 analysis frames x 4-6 tiles ≈ 340k inferences ≈ ~2h of detection alone on a T4), the CPU-bound BoT-SORT GMC pass on 4 vCPU, the unbudgeted full-match annotated render, or cold-start image pull + TensorRT engine build (10-20 billed min/job at scale-from-zero). The 5h timeout caps a runaway job at ~$4.20 — by killing it and refunding, i.e., pure loss. Margin arithmetic: $25 → (25-3.50)/25 = 86% correct as written but omits Stripe fees ($1.03 on $25 → true ~82%); $200/10 → 82.5% correct; $750/50 → 76.7% ≈ 77% correct. FLOOR PRICE FAILS: (8-6.45)/8 = 19.4%, not '>50%' — 50% margin at $6.45 worst-case COGS requires a $12.90 floor. Straight arithmetic error. Storage: raw 6GB at Hot 14d + Cool 76d ≈ $0.20/match, consistent; but the 'tens of MB' results claim is off ~50x — a full-match 720p H.264 preview at 1.5-2.5 Mbps is ~1-1.7GB, kept Hot for the account lifetime (~$0.02-0.03/match/month with unbounded growth: 1,000 matches ≈ 1.4TB Hot ≈ $25+/mo and climbing), and the $0.10 egress allotment covers roughly ONE full preview viewing (1-1.5GB x $0.087/GB) — multiple coaches scrubbing tag review exceeds it. Spot (verified): NC8as_T4_v3 Spot ≈ $0.32/hr, so $0.69/match ≈ 2.2h assuming zero evictions; without checkpointing, evicted 2-3h jobs restart from zero, so realistic Spot COGS is meaningfully above $0.69 and the '~80% COGS cut' is best-case. Bottom line: at $25/match the economics survive even a 2x COGS miss ((25-7-1.03)/25 ≈ 68% margin); they break at the claimed $8 floor, in the preview-clip storage/egress line, and in any world where jobs routinely exceed ~3.7h. Sources: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-apps/billing , https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-apps/gpu-serverless-overview , https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/5823616/azure-container-app-consuption-profile-gpu-cost-ca , https://instances.vantage.sh/azure/vm/nc8ast4-v3 , https://cloudprice.net/vm/Standard_NC8as_T4_v3
missing: 1) Networking for GPU worker → Postgres: the new workload-profile ACA environment is a separate VNet story from the existing backend; firewall/private-endpoint access to managed Postgres is unaddressed. 2) Storage-account CORS configuration for browser direct-to-Blob upload. 3) ACA serverless-GPU quota lead time in westus2 — and the Modal escape hatch contradicts the 'video never leaves Azure' commitment made for the academy/minors story. 4) Multi-file/two-half uploads and trimming — the single raw/{match_id}.mp4 blob assumes one continuous recording, which grassroots footage rarely is. 5) A tagging-time budget and instrumentation — the design's core interaction has no target and no measurement plan. 6) Rights/ToS for uploaded footage: 'their own games or rivals'' invites uploads of broadcast or league-rights-encumbered video; no copyright/abuse policy, no consent basis for named opposition players (Phase D 'opponent scouting packs' heightens this). 7) Queue-wait expectation management: parallelism cap 3 with a lumpy Sunday workload means upload #10 waits 9-12h before its '1-4h' progress bar even starts — no ETA, no priority tiers. 8) Deploy semantics for in-flight 3-4h GPU jobs when the vision image updates (drain vs kill). 9) Monitoring/alerting for the GPU path (failure rates, gpu_seconds drift vs COGS model, queue depth) — gpu_seconds is recorded but nothing consumes it. 10) Decoder threat model: ffmpeg CVE surface on attacker-supplied video inside a credentialed worker — no sandboxing/privilege-separation statement. 11) H.264 patent-pool posture for NVENC-encoded previews distributed commercially (minor but real). 12) Cancellation latency: status checks only at stage boundaries means a cancel can take an hour-plus of billed GPU to take effect.
